what_it_does: A single CSS stylesheet that styles an Imagery subsystem and related tools: pills/banners, tab bars, toolbars, image grids and cards, empty states, project/hub layouts, a 360-degree panorama thumbnail strip and viewer, an AI Studio v3 generative-image interface (prompts, presets, pipelines, source/compose, parameters, results, chat, modal), a mask editor, and a full modal image editor sheet with crop, filters, adjustments, rotate, and AI actions. It extends a warm-paper-with-gold-ink color palette via CSS custom properties.
observations: Class namespaces are split across cv-im-, cv-as-, cv-pano-, cv-mask-, and cv-ed- prefixes.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radius, shadow, font, and easing tokens.
Layout is almost entirely Flexbox and CSS Grid, with sticky side rails and auto-fill image grids.
Includes several animated states: a gold pulse ring on panorama pins, a running pipeline dot pulse, skeleton stripes, and a modal fade-in.
Running/processing states are visualized with repeating diagonal stripe gradients.
The image editor modal is a comprehensive sheet with a crop box and resize handles, rule-of-thirds grid, filter thumbnails, ratio buttons, rotate controls, sliders, and AI result thumbnails.

The AI Studio section is large and includes a resizable shell, preset list, pipeline list, prompt textarea, source dropzone/ picker, compose grid, parameter panel, run bar, results grid, history, and chat interface.
A single media query at 1200px switches the results grid to two columns.
Some selectors hardcode rgba(31,26,18,...) overlays and backdrops rather than using theme variables.
standouts: The file unifies a very broad surface area—asset library, 360 panorama viewer, generative AI studio, mask editor, and image editor—inside one stylesheet.
The warm-paper-with-gold-ink palette is applied consistently across every subsystem, giving the whole file a strong thematic identity.
The image editor modal contains a surprisingly complete set of patterns: crop handles, filter grid, aspect ratios, rotation, sliders, AI result picker, and action footer.
AI Studio v3 uses a deck-restraint redesign with resizable columns, pipeline orchestration UI, and a chat-style composer.
conventions: BEM-ish naming with block prefixes and double-dash modifiers.
CSS custom properties used for theming, spacing, and motion tokens.
Interactive elements have hover transitions and focus rings using the gold accent.
Section comments divide the file into functional areas.
Sticky positioning used for navigation rails.
Reusable tab underline pattern repeated across cv-im-tabs, cv-as-tabs, and cv-ed-modes.
concerns: Some declarations use !important (e.g., .cv-as-param--slider), which breaks the otherwise flat specificity model.
Mix of multiple class prefixes without an obvious single naming convention may make discoverability harder.
Responsive rule uses .cv-resizable-col:nth-child(5), which is fragile if column order changes.
Hardcoded rgba values for the dark overlay color appear in many places instead of a single theme variable.
Z-index values (200, 220) are declared without an apparent stacking scale.
No visible reduced-motion or accessibility-focused media queries beyond basic focus styles.
notes: This is a single, theme-heavy stylesheet that tries to cover the entire imagery/AI editing surface of the app, from browsing assets to generating and editing images in modals.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties such as --paper-2, --s-1, and --accent-gold defined?
Which JavaScript components map to the cv-as-, cv-ed-, cv-mask-, and cv-pano- namespaces?
Is the 360 viewer implemented with WebGL, CSS transforms, or a third-party library?
What does deck-restraint redesign specifically constrain in the AI Studio layout?
How are AI presets and pipelines persisted and validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles imagery, settings, the editor, and the 360 viewer. The file also adds a mask editor and a full modal image editor sheet, so the actual scope is slightly broader than the header summary.
apparent_themes: unified creative asset and generative-AI workspace
progressive disclosure through tabs, rails, and modals
tactile feedback via hover, focus, and motion tokens
content-first image browsing with metadata overlays
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create one coherent, polished visual system that lets users browse, organize, generate, and edit images without leaving a single themed workspace.
novelty: It is unusual for one stylesheet to span a traditional image library, a 360 panorama viewer, a generative AI studio with pipelines and chat, a mask editor, and a full modal image editor, all tied to a distinctive warm-paper/gold-ink palette.
contribution: A comprehensive, themed CSS vocabulary for imagery-related UI patterns—asset grids, project/hub rails, panorama viewer, AI generation controls, mask editing, and a modal image editor—that a fused system could keep as a shared styling layer for creative media workflows.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet defining the warm-paper-and-gold themed UI for an imagery subsystem, including asset grids, project/hub layouts, a 360 panorama viewer, an AI Studio v3 generative interface, a mask editor, and a modal image editor with crop, filters, adjustments, and AI actions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a unified visual and layout system for an imagery workspace that combines asset management, panoramic media, AI-assisted image creation, and manual image editing.
